title: Metacognition and Confidence: A Review and Synthesis.

assets.abstract: Determining the psychological, computational, and neural bases of confidence and uncertainty holds promise for understanding foundational aspects of human metacognition. While a neuroscience of confidence has focused on the mechanisms underpinning subpersonal phenomena such as representations of uncertainty in the visual or motor system, metacognition research has been concerned with personal-level beliefs and knowledge about self-performance. I provide a road map for bridging this divide by foc

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Metacognition — self-awareness, self-monitoring, neural correlates of meta-awareness.
